title: satureja cristata subsp. carminea
intro: Satureja cristata subsp. carminea, also known as Carpathian savory, is a subspecies of the Satureja cristata plant, a member of the mint family. It is native to the Carpathian Mountains region and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Carpathian savory are believed to provide several health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, colds, and flu, as well as anti-cancer and anti-aging effects. Carpathian savory is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin to harness its medicinal properties.
